title: athroandra macrophylla
intro: Athroandra macrophylla, also known as the "Rau Ova", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Borneo, belonging to the family Hernandiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Athroandra macrophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and act as an antiseptic to treat wounds. Athroandra macrophylla can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
